functions.label: getNodeDocumentMode
description:
Returns the node document mode, or 'na' if documentsData is missing or empty.
When this function returns a value other than 'na', documentsData is guaranteed to be a non-empty array.

functions.label: getSingleDocumentData
description:
Returns the single document data for a node if it is in single-* mode.
If the node is not in one of these modes, or if it has no documentsData, it returns undefined.

functions.label: Graph
description:
Graph component renders a graph visualization using ReactFlow.
It takes nodes and edges as input and provides interactive controls
for panning, zooming, and manipulating the graph.

children.label: props
description: - The properties for the Graph component.

functions.label: GraphGroupedNodePreviewPanel
description: Panel to be displayed in the document details expandable flyout on top of right section

functions.label: GraphInvestigation
description: Graph investigation view allows the user to expand nodes and view related entities.

functions.label: hasNodeDocumentsData
description:
Type guard: Returns true if node.documentsData is a non-empty array.
This only narrows node.documentsData to a non-empty array, not to a specific document type.
signature: & { documentsData: [Readonly<{ index?: string | undefined; alert?: Readonly<{ ruleName?: string | undefined; } & {}> | undefined; event?: Readonly<{} & { id: string; }> | undefined; entity?: Readonly<{ type?: string | undefined; name?: string | undefined; host?: Readonly<{ ip?: string[] | undefined; } & {}> | undefined; sub_type?: string | undefined; engine_type?: "user" | "service" | "host" | "generic" | undefined; availableInEntityStore?: boolean | undefined; sourceFields?: Readonly<{} & {}> | undefined; } & {}> | undefined; } & { id: string; type: "alert" | "event" | "entity"; }>, ...Readonly<{ index?: string | undefined; alert?: Readonly<{ ruleName?: string | undefined; } & {}> | undefined; event?: Readonly<{} & { id: string; }> | undefined; entity?: Readonly<{ type?: string | undefined; name?: string | undefined; host?: Readonly<{ ip?: string[] | undefined; } & {}> | undefined; sub_type?: string | undefined; engine_type?: "user" | "service" | "host" | "generic" | undefined; availableInEntityStore?: boolean | undefined; sourceFields?: Readonly<{} & {}> | undefined; } & {}> | undefined; } & { id: string; type: "alert" | "event" | "entity"; }>[]]; }

functions.label: isEntityNodeEnriched
description:
Checks if a node has entity store enrichment.
Only relevant for single-entity mode - returns false for all other modes.
For single-entity nodes, checks if at least one document has entity.availableInEntityStore === true.

functions.label: useFetchGraphData
description: Hook to fetch event's graph visualization data.

children.label: scopeId
description: Unique identifier for the graph instance, used to scope filter state.

children.label: nodes
description: Array of node view models to be rendered in the graph.

children.label: edges
description: Array of edge view models to be rendered in the graph.

children.label: interactive
description:
Determines whether the graph is interactive (allows panning, zooming, etc.).
When set to false, the graph is locked and user interactions are disabled, effectively putting it in view-only mode.

children.label: isLocked
description: Determines whether the graph is locked. Nodes and edges are still interactive, but the graph itself is not.

children.label: showMinimap
description: Determines whether the minimap is visible or not in interactive graphs

children.label: children
description: Additional children to be rendered inside the graph component.

children.label: onCenterGraphAfterRefresh
description:
Callback invoked when the graph is updated with new nodes.
Receives one argument with the list of newly added nodes.
When callback is undefined, graph will center on new nodes (default behavior).
Based on the return value of this callback:
- Returning undefined will center the graph on new nodes (default behavior)
- Returning "fit-view" will fit entire graph into view
- Returning empty array or list of non-existent node ids will keep the graph with its current position
- Returning list of existent node ids will center the graph on those nodes
